articles.title: Valve is so behind on Steam Controller orders that some won't ship until 2027
articles.description: Valve has some good news and bad news about Steam Controllers. The good news: If you make a reservation for a Steam Controller, the company will now show you one of three estimates of when you'll be able to actually order your gamepad: by September 2026, by December 2026, or sometime in 2027. The bad […]
articles.author: Jay Peters

articles.title: You can now use the Game Boy Camera with your phone
articles.description: The $50 GB Operator is an accessory that lets you connect, play, and authenticate Game Boy, Game Boy Color, and Game Boy Advance cartridges on PCs and other devices. Now it's getting some new functionality for the Game Boy Camera. After turning the Game Boy Camera into a charmingly awful desktop webcam two years ago, […]
articles.author: Andrew Liszewski

articles.title: HBO Max's annual plans are 28 percent off right now
articles.description: The easiest way to save on a streaming service is often to pay for a year upfront, which HBO Max is currently making a lot cheaper. Through July 15, 2026, new and returning subscribers can get 28 percent off any of HBO Max's yearly plans, bringing the ad-supported tier down to $78.99 ($31 off) per […]
articles.author: Sheena Vasani

articles.title: Snap’s Specs look good on nobody
articles.description: Snap's new smart glasses are probably the most impressive bit of face-computer technology we've seen. They're not VR-headset huge; they don't have a big charging puck; thanks to Snap's many years of AR lens development, they're likely to have a lot of features right out of the box. (Yes, they're $2,195, but that may just […]
articles.author: David Pierce

articles.title: The best early Amazon Prime Day deals so far
articles.description: Amazon’s earlier-than-usual Prime Day doesn’t begin until June 23rd, but there are several even earlier deals on must-have products that you can check out right now. To name some examples, Apple’s AirPods Pro 3 are selling at their cheapest-ever price at Amazon and Walmart (though, the sale keeps playing peek-a-boo). Also, you can grab Ring’s […]
articles.author: Cameron Faulkner

articles.title: This robotic self-driving toilet comes to you
articles.description: During a recent expo in Shanghai that focuses on elderly care, assistive devices, and rehabilitation medicine, a Chinese company called Yueban debuted a smart toilet that does something we haven't seen before; it comes to you. The autonomous self-driving Xiaoban toilet was designed to improve accessibility for those dealing with mobility issues due to age, […]
articles.author: Andrew Liszewski

articles.title: A year of Walmart Plus is half off ahead of Prime Day
articles.description: Do you often find yourself shopping at the big blue, or perhaps you’re just looking for an alternative to Amazon? Either way, Walmart is currently offering a year of its Walmart Plus subscription for $49, which typically costs $98. The membership includes perks like free delivery on orders over $35, free next-day and two-day shipping […]
articles.author: Brad Bourque

articles.title: Firefox’s new home page widgets are helping me focus
articles.description: I launched Firefox this morning to find some new blocks on my home page. The widgets that are currently rolling out add sports scores, time zones, a focus timer, and a checklist, which are already some of my favorite new Firefox features in years. I usually have Focus Friend open on my phone when I […]
articles.author: Stevie Bonifield

articles.title: The Onion’s rebooted InfoWars is coming July 2nd
articles.description: The Onion's InfoWars officially has a launch date: On July 2nd, the conspiracy network previously run by Alex Jones will return as a comedy and media platform. The reboot comes more than a year and a half after news broke that the satirical news site was working to acquire the property owned by Jones, a […]
articles.author: Mia Sato

articles.title: Midjourney goes from generating cat images to full-body ultrasound scans
articles.description: Midjourney CEO David Holz just showed off the company's first hardware product and plans to build a San Francisco spa, which he admitted is a bit different from the "cat pictures" produced by its AI image generator. Dubbed The Midjourney Scanner, it's an ultrasound-based full-body scanner that uses a ring of sensors to capture vertical […]
articles.author: Richard Lawler
